title: phlegmariurus hamiltonii var. petiolatus
intro: Phlegmariurus hamiltonii var. petiolatus, also known as the creeping fern, is a species of plant in the family Lycopodiaceae, characterized by its small, creeping stems and compound leaves. The creeping fern has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the creeping fern is often used as a decoction or infusion, with the dried stems and leaves being steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
